name: p_6_1_a_process_array_even_indexed_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that takes an array of ten integers and then returns every element at an even index.

name: p_6_1_a_process_array_even_indexed_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that takes an array of ten integers and then returns every element at an even index.

name: p_6_1_b_process_array_every_even_element_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that takes an array of ten integers and then returns every even element.

name: p_6_1_b_process_array_every_even_element_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that takes an array of ten integers and then returns every even element.

name: p_6_1_c_process_array_reverse_order_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that takes an array of ten integers and then returns all elements in reverse order.

ensures: forall i :: 0 <= i < |reversed| ==> reversed[i] == inputs[|inputs| - 1 - i]

name: p_6_1_c_process_array_reverse_order_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that takes an array of ten integers and then returns all elements in reverse order.

ensures: forall i :: 0 <= i < reversed.Length ==> reversed[i] == inputs[inputs.Length - 1 - i]

name: p_6_1_d_process_array_first_and_last_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that takes an array of ten integers and then returns the first and last elements.

name: p_6_1_d_process_array_first_and_last_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that takes an array of ten integers and then returns the first and last elements.

name: p_6_2_a_swap_first_last_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to swap the first and last elements in an array.

name: p_6_2_a_swap_first_last_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to swap the first and last elements in an array.

ensures: forall i :: 1 <= i < inputs.Length - 1 ==> result[i] == inputs[i]

name: p_6_2_b_shift_right_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to shift all elements of an array by one to the right and move the last element into the first position. For example, [1, 4, 9, 16, 25] would be transformed into [25, 1, 4, 9, 16].

name: p_6_2_b_shift_right_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to shift all elements of an array by one to the right and move the last element into the first position. For example, [1, 4, 9, 16, 25] would be transformed into [25, 1, 4, 9, 16].

ensures: forall i :: 1 <= i < inputs.Length ==> result[i] == inputs[i - 1]

name: p_6_2_c_replace_even_with_zero_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to replace all even elements in an array with 0.

ensures: forall i :: 0 <= i < |inputs| ==> (inputs[i] % 2 == 0 ==> result[i] == 0) && (inputs[i] % 2 != 0 ==> result[i] == inputs[i])

name: p_6_2_c_replace_even_with_zero_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to replace all even elements in an array with 0.

ensures: forall i :: 0 <= i < inputs.Length ==> (inputs[i] % 2 == 0 ==> result[i] == 0) && (inputs[i] % 2 != 0 ==> result[i] == inputs[i])

name: p_6_2_d_replace_with_larger_neighbor_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to replace each element of an array, except the first and last, by the larger of its two neighbors.

ensures: forall i :: 1 <= i < |inputs| - 1 ==> result[i] == if inputs[i - 1] >= inputs[i + 1] then inputs[i - 1] else inputs[i + 1]

name: p_6_2_d_replace_with_larger_neighbor_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to replace each element of an array, except the first and last, by the larger of its two neighbors.

ensures: forall i :: 1 <= i < inputs.Length - 1 ==> result[i] == if inputs[i - 1] >= inputs[i + 1] then inputs[i - 1] else inputs[i + 1]

name: p_6_2_e_remove_middle_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to remove the middle element of an array if the array length is odd, or the middle two elements if the length is even.

ensures: |inputs| % 2 == 1 ==> forall i :: 0 <= i < |inputs| / 2 ==> result[i] == inputs[i] && forall j :: |inputs| / 2 <= j < |result| ==> result[j] == inputs[j + 1]
|inputs| % 2 == 0 ==> forall i :: 0 <= i < |inputs| / 2 - 1 ==> result[i] == inputs[i] && forall j :: |inputs| / 2 - 1 <= j < |result| ==> result[j] == inputs[j + 2]

name: p_6_2_e_remove_middle_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to remove the middle element of an array if the array length is odd, or the middle two elements if the length is even.

ensures: result.Length == if inputs.Length % 2 == 1 then inputs.Length - 1 else inputs.Length - 2
inputs.Length % 2 == 1 ==> forall i :: 0 <= i < inputs.Length / 2 ==> result[i] == inputs[i] && forall j :: inputs.Length / 2 <= j < result.Length ==> result[j] == inputs[j + 1]
inputs.Length % 2 == 0 ==> forall i :: 0 <= i < inputs.Length / 2 - 1 ==> result[i] == inputs[i] && forall j :: inputs.Length / 2 - 1 <= j < result.Length ==> result[j] == inputs[j + 2]

name: p_6_2_f_move_even_to_front_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to move all even elements in an array to the front, otherwise preserving the order of the elements.

name: p_6_2_f_move_even_to_front_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to move all even elements in an array to the front, otherwise preserving the order of the elements.

name: p_6_2_g_second_largest_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to return the second-largest element in an array.

ensures: forall x :: x in inputs ==> x < find_largest(inputs) ==> x <= second_largest

name: p_6_2_g_second_largest_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to return the second-largest element in an array.

ensures: forall x :: x in inputs[..] ==> x < find_largest(inputs[..]) ==> x <= second_largest

name: p_6_2_h_is_sorted_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to return true if the given array is currently sorted in increasing order.

name: p_6_2_h_is_sorted_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to return true if the given array is currently sorted in increasing order.

ensures: is_sorted == (forall i :: 0 <= i < inputs.Length - 1 ==> inputs[i] <= inputs[i + 1])

name: p_6_2_i_has_adjacent_duplicates_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to return true if the given array contains two adjacent duplicate elements.

name: p_6_2_i_has_adjacent_duplicates_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to return true if the given array contains two adjacent duplicate elements.

ensures: has_duplicates == (exists i :: 0 <= i < inputs.Length - 1 && inputs[i] == inputs[i + 1])

name: p_6_2_j_has_duplicates_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to return true if the given array contains duplicate elements (which need not be adjacent).

ensures: has_duplicates == (exists i, j :: 0 <= i < j < |inputs| && inputs[i] == inputs[j])

name: p_6_2_j_has_duplicates_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method to return true if the given array contains duplicate elements (which need not be adjacent).

ensures: has_duplicates == (exists i, j :: 0 <= i < j < inputs.Length && inputs[i] == inputs[j])

name: p_6_4_sum_without_smallest_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that computes the sum of an array of values, except for the smallest one, in a single loop. In the loop, update the sum and the smallest value. After the loop, return the difference.

name: p_6_4_sum_without_smallest_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that computes the sum of an array of values, except for the smallest one, in a single loop. In the loop, update the sum and the smallest value. After the loop, return the difference.

name: p_6_6_alternating_sum_seq
nat_lang_statement: Compute the alternating sum of all elements in an array. For example, if your method reads the input [1, 4, 9, 16, 9, 7, 4, 9, 11] then it computes 1 – 4 + 9 – 16 + 9 – 7 + 4 – 9 + 11 = –2.

name: p_6_6_alternating_sum_array
nat_lang_statement: Compute the alternating sum of all elements in an array. For example, if your method reads the input [1, 4, 9, 16, 9, 7, 4, 9, 11] then it computes 1 – 4 + 9 – 16 + 9 – 7 + 4 – 9 + 11 = –2.

name: p_6_7_reverse_array_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that reverses the sequence of elements in an array. For example, if you call the method with the array [1, 4, 9, 16, 9, 7, 4, 9, 11] then the array is changed to [11, 9, 4, 7, 9, 16, 9, 4, 1].

ensures: forall i :: 0 <= i < |reversed| ==> reversed[i] == inputs[|inputs| - 1 - i]

name: p_6_7_reverse_array_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that reverses the sequence of elements in an array. For example, if you call the method with the array [1, 4, 9, 16, 9, 7, 4, 9, 11] then the array is changed to [11, 9, 4, 7, 9, 16, 9, 4, 1].

ensures: forall i :: 0 <= i < reversed.Length ==> reversed[i] == inputs[inputs.Length - 1 - i]

name: p_6_9_arrays_equal_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that checks whether two arrays have the same elements in the same order.

dafny.method_signature: p_6_9_arrays_equal_seq(first: seq<int>, second: seq<int>) returns (are_equal: bool)
ensures: are_equal == (|first| == |second| && forall i :: 0 <= i < |first| ==> first[i] == second[i])

name: p_6_9_arrays_equal_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that checks whether two arrays have the same elements in the same order.

dafny.method_signature: p_6_9_arrays_equal_array(first: array<int>, second: array<int>) returns (are_equal: bool)
ensures: are_equal == (first.Length == second.Length && forall i :: 0 <= i < first.Length ==> first[i] == second[i])

name: p_6_10_same_set_seq
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that checks whether two arrays have the same elements in some order, ignoring duplicates.

dafny.method_signature: p_6_10_same_set_seq(first: seq<int>, second: seq<int>) returns (are_same_set: bool)

name: p_6_10_same_set_array
nat_lang_statement: Write a method that checks whether two arrays have the same elements in some order, ignoring duplicates.

dafny.method_signature: p_6_10_same_set_array(first: array<int>, second: array<int>) returns (are_same_set: bool)
